seoTitle: How to Delete a Bill in QuickBooks Online Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to delete an incorrect bill in QuickBooks Online and avoid common mistakes that can throw off your accounts payable records.
intro: Mistaken or duplicate bills can throw off your accounts payable and make your financial reports less reliable. This guide covers the steps to delete a bill in QuickBooks Online correctly, plus mistakes to avoid, whether you're a small business owner correcting an entry or a bookkeeper cleaning up a client's file.

steps.heading: Access the bills section
steps.body: Go to the bills area of QuickBooks Online to find the specific bill that needs to be removed.

steps.heading: Open the bill you want to delete
steps.body: Select the bill and review its details to confirm it's the correct one before making any changes.

steps.heading: Delete the bill
steps.body: Use the delete option to remove the bill from your accounts payable records.

steps.heading: Watch for common mistakes
steps.body: Avoid deleting a bill that's already been partially paid without first reviewing the associated payment, since that can leave your records inconsistent.

steps.heading: Review your accounts payable afterward
steps.body: Check your vendor balances and reports to make sure the deletion is reflected accurately across your books.

faq.question: What happens if I delete a bill that's already been paid?
faq.answer: Deleting a paid bill can leave the associated payment unmatched, so it's best to review or unlink the payment first.

faq.question: Can I undo deleting a bill in QuickBooks Online?
faq.answer: There's no simple undo button, but the audit log can help you see what was deleted so you can recreate the bill if needed.

faq.question: Is it better to delete or void a bill?
faq.answer: Voiding keeps a record of the transaction while zeroing it out, which is often preferable for audit purposes over an outright deletion.

faq.question: Do I need accounting experience to delete a bill?
faq.answer: No, the process itself is straightforward in QuickBooks Online, though understanding the impact on your books is helpful before doing it.
